conjecture_title: Informal conjecture on sun-free $\chi$-boundedness without net exclusion
paper_title: Suns in triangle-free graphs of large chromatic number
summary: The conjecture that the class of all sun-free graphs is $\chi$-bounded was posed informally in arXiv:2506.10227, motivated by the authors' uncertainty about whether net-freeness is genuinely needed alongside sun-freeness. The main result of the paper proves that every triangle-free graph of chromatic number at least 48 contains an induced $t$-sun ($t\ge 5$) or a near-4-sun, giving structural leverage but not settling $\chi$-boundedness of sun-free graphs. No follow-up paper resolving or refuting this informal conjecture was found in the literature through May 2026.
search_queries: sun-free graphs chi-bounded conjecture Hajebi Spirkl 2025
notes: The conjecture is informal (a remark after Problem 1.4) and attributed to the paper's authors. It is closely related to the formal Problem 1.4 (χ-boundedness of (sun,net)-free graphs). The Barbados 2026 open-problems document was located but could not be parsed for additional context. No evidence of resolution found.
